top3.title: Texas SB 2420 Comes Into Effect: What Companies Need to Know About AI Disclosure Obligations
top3.title_cn: Texas SB 2420 Comes Into Effect: What Companies Need to Know About AI Disclosure Obligations
top3.summary: Texas Senate Bill 2420, signed June 22, 2025, takes effect today, May 6, 2026. The law requires any business operating in Texas to disclose to consumers when AI is used to make 'consequential decisions' affecting employment, housing, healthcare, or financial services. The Texas Attorney General has been given enforcement authority with civil penalties up to $7,500 per violation.
top3.reason: [执法事实] Inside Privacy (Covington) · 风险冒头期 · 无焦虑点命中

pool.title: FTC to Ban Kochava and Subsidiary from Selling Sensitive Location Data to Settle Charges They Sold Location Data Linked to Millions of Mobile Devices
pool.title_cn: FTC禁止Kochava出售敏感位置数据以和解指控
pool.summary: Data could be used to trace movements of individuals, FTC alleged The Federal Trade Commission will prohibit data broker Kochava and its subsidiary from selling, sharing or disclosing sensitive location data without consumers’ affirmative express consent to settle allegations the companies sold location data from hundreds of millions of mobile devices that could be used to trace the movements of individuals. View Press Release

pool.title: The GPAI Code and the Myth of European AI Leadership
pool.title_cn: The GPAI Code and the Myth of European AI Leadership
pool.summary: Behind the celebratory press releases lies a sobering reality: the EU has neither the compute, the talent, nor the capital to lead AI governance through regulatory instruments alone. The new GPAI Code is a well-crafted document — and a politically convenient one — but it cannot substitute for industrial policy. Brussels risks regulating an industry it does not have.
pool.reason: [官方文件] Tech Policy Press · 风险冒头期 · 无焦虑点命中

pool.title: EU Commission publishes final GPAI Code of Practice
pool.title_cn: EU Commission publishes final GPAI Code of Practice
pool.summary: On May 4, 2026, the European Commission officially published the final version of the General-Purpose AI Code of Practice under the EU AI Act. Effective from August 2, 2026, the Code requires GPAI providers to disclose training data sources, conduct systemic risk assessments, and report serious incidents within 15 days. Signatories include OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Mistral.
pool.reason: [立法事实] DataGuidance (KtN) · 风险冒头期 · 无焦虑点命中

pool.title: Ofcom Launches Second Wave of Illegal Harms Code Enforcement: Five Platforms Under Investigation
pool.title_cn: Ofcom Launches Second Wave of Illegal Harms Code Enforcement: Five Platforms Under Investigation
pool.summary: The UK communications regulator Ofcom announced on May 5, 2026, the opening of formal investigations against five major user-to-user services for failures under the Online Safety Act's Illegal Harms Codes of Practice. Affected platforms include two adult content sites, two messaging apps, and one image-sharing service. Maximum penalties under the Act reach 10% of global annual turnover.

top3.title: Why the EU's GPAI Code of Practice Won't Solve the Frontier Risk Problem
top3.title_cn: Why the EU's GPAI Code of Practice Won't Solve the Frontier Risk Problem
top3.summary: The EU's final GPAI Code of Practice, while celebrated as the world's first binding industry code for frontier AI, suffers from three structural flaws: first, the systemic risk threshold of 10^25 FLOPs is already obsolete; second, the third-party audit clause lacks teeth; third, signatories retain unilateral exit rights. We argue the Code is more a political signal than a regulatory instrument.
top3.reason: [观点分析] Lawfare · 风险冒头期 · 无焦虑点命中

pool.title: Agentic AI and the Coming Liability Mess: Why 2026 Is the Year of the Lawsuit
pool.title_cn: Agentic AI and the Coming Liability Mess: Why 2026 Is the Year of the Lawsuit
pool.summary: Anthropic's Claude can now operate a computer. OpenAI's Operator can buy you tickets. Google's Project Mariner browses the web on your behalf. None of the existing legal frameworks — agency law, product liability, consumer protection — were written with autonomous AI in mind. I predict 2026 will see at least three landmark lawsuits that will force courts to choose a doctrine. Here's my bet on which.
pool.reason: [观点分析] Import AI (Substack) · 落地执行期 · Agent 等新形态

top3.title: ICO Publishes Final Age Assurance Guidance for Online Services
top3.title_cn: ICO Publishes Final Age Assurance Guidance for Online Services
top3.summary: The UK Information Commissioner's Office released its final Age Assurance Guidance on May 2, 2026, providing detailed expectations for online services likely to be accessed by children. The guidance establishes a 'risk-proportionate' standard, recommends specific age estimation methods, and requires DPIAs for any age assurance system processing biometric data.

pool.title: California Senate Bill 1047 Signed: AI Companion Apps Must Implement Suicide Prevention Protocols by January 2027
pool.title_cn: California Senate Bill 1047 Signed: AI Companion Apps Must Implement Suicide Prevention Protocols by January 2027
pool.summary: California Governor signed SB 1047 into law on May 3, 2026, establishing the nation's first state-level requirements for AI companion applications. The law mandates suicide-prevention prompts when minors discuss self-harm, requires age verification for accounts, and imposes a $10,000-per-incident civil penalty for violations. Effective January 1, 2027.
pool.reason: [立法事实] DataGuidance (KtN) · 落地执行期 · 未成年人极端事件

top3.title: Why the EU's GPAI Code of Practice Won't Solve the Frontier Risk Problem
top3.title_cn: Why the EU's GPAI Code of Practice Won't Solve the Frontier Risk Problem
top3.summary: The EU's final GPAI Code of Practice, while celebrated as the world's first binding industry code for frontier AI, suffers from three structural flaws: first, the systemic risk threshold of 10^25 FLOPs is already obsolete; second, the third-party audit clause lacks teeth; third, signatories retain unilateral exit rights. We argue the Code is more a political signal than a regulatory instrument.
top3.reason: [观点分析] Lawfare · 风险冒头期 · 无焦虑点命中

pool.title: Why AI Watermarking Is Failing — And What Should Replace It
pool.title_cn: Why AI Watermarking Is Failing — And What Should Replace It
pool.summary: Three years after the major labs committed to robust watermarking under the White House voluntary commitments, independent audits show 80%+ removal rates with simple post-processing. The C2PA standard fares slightly better but suffers from low adoption. We argue the entire 'detect AI content' paradigm is misconceived, and propose a shift to provenance-by-design at the hardware layer.
pool.reason: [观点分析] AI Snake Oil · 风险冒头期 · 标识失效

pool.title: Agentic AI and the Coming Liability Mess: Why 2026 Is the Year of the Lawsuit
pool.title_cn: Agentic AI and the Coming Liability Mess: Why 2026 Is the Year of the Lawsuit
pool.summary: Anthropic's Claude can now operate a computer. OpenAI's Operator can buy you tickets. Google's Project Mariner browses the web on your behalf. None of the existing legal frameworks — agency law, product liability, consumer protection — were written with autonomous AI in mind. I predict 2026 will see at least three landmark lawsuits that will force courts to choose a doctrine. Here's my bet on which.
pool.reason: [观点分析] Import AI (Substack) · 落地执行期 · Agent 等新形态

pool.title: The GPAI Code and the Myth of European AI Leadership

pool.summary: Behind the celebratory press releases lies a sobering reality: the EU has neither the compute, the talent, nor the capital to lead AI governance through regulatory instruments alone. The new GPAI Code is a well-crafted document — and a politically convenient one — but it cannot substitute for industrial policy. Brussels risks regulating an industry it does not have.

pool.title: EU Commission publishes final GPAI Code of Practice

pool.summary: On May 4, 2026, the European Commission officially published the final version of the General-Purpose AI Code of Practice under the EU AI Act. Signatories include OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Mistral.

pool.title: Why the EU's GPAI Code of Practice Won't Solve the Frontier Risk Problem
pool.title_cn: Why the EU's GPAI Code of Practice Won't Solve the Frontier Risk Problem
pool.summary: The EU's final GPAI Code of Practice, while celebrated as the world's first binding industry code for frontier AI, suffers from three structural flaws: first, the systemic risk threshold of 10^25 FLOPs is already obsolete; second, the third-party audit clause lacks teeth; third, signatories retain unilateral exit rights. We argue the Code is more a political signal than a regulatory instrument.
pool.reason: [观点分析] Lawfare · 风险冒头期 · 无焦虑点命中

pool.title: Why AI Watermarking Is Failing — And What Should Replace It
pool.title_cn: Why AI Watermarking Is Failing — And What Should Replace It
pool.summary: Three years after the major labs committed to robust watermarking under the White House voluntary commitments, independent audits show 80%+ removal rates with simple post-processing. The C2PA standard fares slightly better but suffers from low adoption. We argue the entire 'detect AI content' paradigm is misconceived, and propose a shift to provenance-by-design at the hardware layer.

pool.summary: Anthropic's Claude can now operate a computer. OpenAI's Operator can buy you tickets. Google's Project Mariner browses the web on your behalf. I predict 2026 will see at least three landmark lawsuits that will force courts to choose a doctrine. Here's my bet on which.

main.title: EU Commission publishes final GPAI Code of Practice
main.title_cn: EU Commission publishes final GPAI Code of Practice
main.summary: On May 4, 2026, the European Commission officially published the final version of the General-Purpose AI Code of Practice under the EU AI Act. Effective from August 2, 2026, the Code requires GPAI providers to disclose training data sources, conduct systemic risk assessments, and report serious incidents within 15 days. Signatories include OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Mistral.
main.reason: [立法事实] DataGuidance (KtN) · 风险冒头期 · 无焦虑点命中

main.title: The Quiet Rise of Agent Liability Regimes: A Comparative Analysis
main.title_cn: The Quiet Rise of Agent Liability Regimes: A Comparative Analysis
main.summary: While headlines focus on the EU AI Act and US executive orders, a quieter regulatory front is opening: dedicated liability regimes for autonomous AI agents. Singapore's MDA, the UK's Law Commission, and Japan's METI have all issued consultation papers in the past 60 days. We compare three emerging models: principal-agent attribution, manufacturer strict liability, and the 'electronic person' approach.
main.reason: [官方文件] IAPP · 讨论立法期 · 无焦虑点命中
